criteria.criterion: A completion PASS returns a `criteria[]` array with one met-record per evaluated criterion and `findings` stays empty, and `reconcile_verdict` does NOT flip it to FAIL. Oracle: a unit test on `reconcile_verdict` feeding a PASS verdict with a populated `criteria[]`, asserting it is NOT flipped to FAIL and the array survives reconcile; plus a test that the `CompletionVerdict` pydantic model round-trips `criteria[]`.

A completion PASS emits a `completion_verifier_pass_v1` sidecar persisting the per-criterion array, read back by `latest_pass_record`. Oracle: an integration test that closes a ticket through the gate with a stub verifier returning the new PASS shape and asserts on the emitted event JSON (via the real sidecar emit path), plus that `latest_pass_record` reads it back and `latest_fail_verdict` returns None on the same ticket.

Live end-to-end: running the real completion verifier (`[agents]` extra + API key) on a fixture ticket whose criteria are met produces a PASS with a populated `criteria[]` persisted to the sidecar. Oracle: a live/E2E test exercising the real agent path, gated on LLM availability (skipped with a recorded reason otherwise).

citation.description: All implementation files (contracts.py, completion_sidecar.py, completion.py, transition_close.py, gate_ops.py, completion-verification.yaml, completion_reconcile_input.schema.json, completion_verdict.schema.json, completion_verifier.md) are fully implemented with no stubs or skips; all new tests in test_audit_completion_v2.py and test_completion_gate.py are concrete with no xfail/skip markers.

criteria.criterion: `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
